title: reoccurring todo items
author: TheMadLorax
body: i have one ask; reoccurring todo list items... i use journals plugin and have 5 shelves with dozens of journals for my fish tank, vinegar fermenting, garden beds, etc where i write todo list items. i dont like using one note for todos; thats just extra work and makes the one note super long. so, i built a bridge between todo and tasks plugin; i write the dataview format [repeat] [onCompletion] [due::] and the bridge watches for [due::]  and inserts a #2001-09-11 afterwards; the next date pops up automatically in the todo pane. can we please have a reoccurring todo item, in whatever fashion you choose?

title: Failed to load ToDos into Panel every next day.
author: LuzianHahn
body:
Hi, 
Thanks for your plugin - it makes my live much easier!

I recently found that after opening obsidian and checking for ToDos in the morning of the next day, the sidebar panel is empty. 
I followed your instruction from #45 to open the developer panel, which indicates an exception. I appended a respective picture here:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a7aca077-2152-4d26-ab3b-608ee573826a)

A restart of obsidian ordinary fixes this. The issues happens on MacOS (Sonoma 14.7.1 - Obsidian 1.7.7) and Ubuntu (Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS - Obsidian 1.7.6), so I guess it is independent from the OS.

title: New plugin name requirements
author: joethei
body:
Hi @larslockefeer

Per our [developer policies](https://docs.obsidian.md/Developer+policies),
please ensure that your plugin's name does not include the word "Obsidian".
In addition, the plugin name should not include the word "Plugin", as that is unecessary duplication.
We have already modified the name of your plugin to "TODO | Text-based GTD" in our records.

To maintain compliance, take the following actions:

1. Modify the manifest.json file in your plugin repository.
2. Generate a new release for your plugin to ensure users download the updated manifest.

If you have a idea for a different plugin name, you may also submit a pull request to the [obsidianmd/obsidian-releases](https://github.com/obsidianmd/obsidian-releases) repository.

Thank you for your cooperation.
— the Obsidian team

title: Tasks in today view not inheriting due date.
author: vascowhite
body:
Hi,

Thanks for this plugin, I find it really useful.

In the listed features you have:

> - Integrates with the Daily Notes plugin: TODOs without a due date will inherit the date of the daily note as due date

This isn't working for me, is there a setting somewhere that I'm missing?

The tasks show up in the 'inbox' section, but I thought they would appear in the 'scheduled' and 'today' views.

author: chrisgmbishop
body:
Looks like there's an issue onLoad.
From what I can tell there is an error at TriggerIndex. Nothing is loading in the ToDo pane.

Hopefully the above trace is helpful.

Thanks in advance!

title: FR: Collect only from tagged files (TODOs from 4000 markdown files in (27.085s)`)
author: gerroon
body:
Hi

This plugin is great but not so great with large vaults

`[obsidian-plugin-todo] Parsed 30 TODOs from 4000 markdown files in (27.085s)`


It would be great if it can limit the search to tagged files like "#todo" etc. Maybe that can improve the load times.

title: Plugin seems to ignore the "excluded files" list
author: andremueller
body: After changing my Options/Files & Links/Excluded files in Obisidian 1.4.16 (macOS) and restarting Obsidian all tasks found in subfolders of the excluded files are shown. So the todo plugin seems to ignore the excluded files section.

title: Main Page that's shown in the ReadMe doesn't exist.
author: BrandonDRoy
body: There is no helpful indications in the ReadMe docs that provide information about how to get the actual page. Unfortunately this is barely usable due to that.

title: Don't delete date tag after mark entry as done
author: ghost
body:
When mark an entry in the todo toolbar view the checkbox will be marked but also the date tag will be deleted.
```markdown
- [ ] a point on my todo list @{2023-04-03}
```
After mark the entry in the todo toolbar view _Today_
```markdown
- [X] a point on my todo list
```

I look for an option to disable the deleting of the date tag.

_@{YYYY-MM-DD} is my custom defined format for the due date in the task description._

title: Please Enable various-complements autocomplete on the popup, "Create or edit Task"
author: socal0001
body:
RelevantPlugin = https://github.com/tadashi-aikawa/obsidian-various-complements-plugin
PopUp = "Create or Edit Task" popup from obsidian-plugin-todo

I have found the RelevantPlugin to speed my work by automcompleting things like tags.  I like to tag my Tasks.  The RelevantPlugin does not seem to work on the Popup.  I do not know whether to ask the RelevantPlugin developer or Tasks developer.

If possible, please enable the RelevantPlugin to work on the Popup or tell me to ask the developer of the RelevantPlugin.

Thanks for the absolutely amazing plugin, humbled and grateful!

title: [Feature Request] Add compatibility with the Obsidian List Callouts plugin
author: minermaniac447
body: I don't quite know how difficult this would be to implement, but I would love to see some sort of integration with the todos panel and the [List Callouts Plugin](https://github.com/mgmeyers/obsidian-list-callouts). That plugin makes it very easy to categorize todo items visually in a single note, and I would love to see some sort of linking between the two plugins so that categorization can carry over to TODO's aggregate todo list.
